message:
fix(lib/status.sh): add missing _status_resolve_explicit_target_home helper

Follow-up to commit 823512c7 which promoted the explicit-ACFS_HOME /
explicit-TARGET_HOME override block above the SUDO_USER probe in
`_status_resolve_acfs_home()`. That reordering referenced
`_status_resolve_explicit_target_home` — a helper the six other
subsystems (changelog, cheatsheet, continue, dashboard, export-
config, support) already defined but status.sh did not. The
reordered block silently no-op'd on status's resolver whenever
`TARGET_HOME` or `TARGET_USER` was set, because the helper returned
"command not found" straight through `|| true`.

Add the helper with the same shape every other subsystem uses:
  1. prefer the sanitized `_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_HOME` value
     (already canonicalized at source time).
  2. otherwise, resolve a valid `_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_USER_RAW`
     through `_status_home_for_user` and validate the result is an
     existing absolute home.
  3. otherwise, return 1 so the caller falls through to the next
     branch.

With the helper present the test added in the prior commit ("ACFS
home resolvers honor explicit TARGET_HOME over stale system state")
now exercises status.sh through the same passing path as the
other six resolvers.

files.patch:
@@ -373,6 +373,26 @@ _status_home_for_user() {
     return 1
 }
 
+_status_resolve_explicit_target_home() {
+    local target_home=""
+
+    target_home="$_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_HOME"
+    if [[ -n "$target_home" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "${target_home%/}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_USER_RAW" ]]; then
+        _status_is_valid_username "$_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_USER_RAW" || return 1
+        target_home="$(_status_existing_abs_home "$(_status_home_for_user "$_STATUS_EXPLICIT_TARGET_USER_RAW" 2>/dev/null || true)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        [[ -n "$target_home" ]] || return 1
+        printf '%s\n' "${target_home%/}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
 _status_read_state_string() {
     local state_file="$1"
     local key="$2"
